vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded test credentials (alice@test.com / password123). While archived, this pattern demonstrates weak security practices.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use environment variables or secure credential management. Remove from version control history.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded HTTP API endpoint without HTTPS. Production must enforce HTTPS for all credential/data transmission.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use environment variables for API endpoints, enforce HTTPS in production configuration

vulnerabilities.description: Command execution via execSync with string concatenation. While temp files mitigate some shell injection, pattern is fragile.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use array-based execution: execSync(cmd, args) instead of string concat

vulnerabilities.description: JSON.parse on WebSocket data without try-catch. Malformed JSON will crash application.
vulnerabilities.fix: Wrap parse in try-catch, validate message structure before processing

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded test password (password123). Even development seeds should not contain weak credentials.
vulnerabilities.fix: Generate random secure passwords in seed script

vulnerabilities.description: Weak hardcoded password (pwd12345) for E2E test users. Should be cryptographically random.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() to generate secure test passwords

vulnerabilities.description: Session IDs use Date.now() for entropy - predictable and not cryptographically random.
API calls cached with NetworkFirst strategy. Sensitive user data could leak from browser cache.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use NetworkOnly for auth/profile endpoints. Implement cache invalidation strategy.

vulnerabilities.description: DOM element access without existence checks. Could throw errors if elements missing.
RegExp patterns constructed from input without anchors. Could match unintended strings.

recommendations.title: Enforce HTTPS for all production APIs
recommendations.description: Configure backend to reject HTTP connections. Implement HSTS headers. Verify SSL/TLS certificates are valid.
recommendations.impact: Prevents credential interception and man-in-the-middle attacks

recommendations.title: Remove all hardcoded credentials from codebase
recommendations.description: Even archived files should not contain real or demo credentials. Use secure credential injection.
recommendations.impact: Eliminates risk of credential exposure via version control

recommendations.title: Implement input validation for all external data
recommendations.description: Use Zod/TypeScript for request validation. Wrap JSON.parse in error handlers. Validate WebSocket messages.
recommendations.impact: Prevents injection attacks and application crashes

recommendations.title: Use cryptographically secure random generation
recommendations.description: Replace Date.now() with crypto.randomBytes(). Generate secure session IDs and tokens.

recommendations.title: Configure service worker cache security
recommendations.description: Use NetworkOnly strategy for sensitive endpoints (/auth/*, /profile/*, /balance/*). Implement cache versioning.
recommendations.impact: Prevents stale sensitive data from being served from cache

recommendations.title: Add rate limiting to authentication endpoints
recommendations.description: Implement rate limiting on /login, /register, /forgot-password. Use express-rate-limit or similar.
recommendations.impact: Prevents brute force and DoS attacks on auth

recommendations.title: Implement CSRF protection
recommendations.description: Add CSRF tokens to state-changing requests. Use SameSite cookies. Verify Origin headers.

recommendations.title: Add Content Security Policy headers
recommendations.description: Implement CSP to restrict script/image/style sources. Start with report-only mode.
recommendations.impact: Defense-in-depth against XSS and injection attacks

recommendations.title: Implement security logging and monitoring
recommendations.description: Log authentication failures, unauthorized access attempts, suspicious API patterns. Never log passwords.

recommendations.title: Add dependency vulnerability scanning
recommendations.description: Use npm audit, Snyk, or similar in CI/CD. Block deployments with critical vulnerabilities.
recommendations.impact: Early detection of known vulnerabilities in dependencies

hardcodedSecrets.risk: Even though in dev/test code, establishes poor security culture

sqlInjection.details: Using Prisma ORM with parameterized queries throughout

locations: scripts/helper.js:30 uses textContent (safe) but lacks validation
Service worker processes untrusted WebSocket data without sanitization
xssVulnerabilities.risk: Low risk due to use of textContent, but missing input validation

issues: No visible rate limiting on auth endpoints

insecureTransport.details: Demo script uses http://localhost:3000 without HTTPS enforcement

dependencyVulnerabilities.details: Run: npm audit in both src/backend and src/frontend
dependencyVulnerabilities.recommendation: Integrate npm audit into CI/CD pipeline

securityBestPractices.authentication: ✗ No visible rate limiting, ✗ Predictable session IDs, ✓ JWT with refresh tokens
securityBestPractices.dataProtection: ✓ bcrypt for passwords, ✗ Weak test credentials, ? No field-level encryption visible
securityBestPractices.transport: ✗ HTTP in demo code, ? HTTPS enforcement not documented
securityBestPractices.validation: ✓ Zod on backend, ✗ Insufficient on frontend/helpers
securityBestPractices.secrets: ✗ Hardcoded in code, ✓ Not in .env (appears to use env vars for prod)

nextSteps: 1. Audit package.json for vulnerable dependencies (npm audit)
3. Add rate limiting middleware to Express server
4. Replace Date.now() with crypto.randomBytes() for all tokens
5. Add input validation using Zod for all external data sources
7. Add security logging for auth failures and suspicious activity
10. Set up automated dependency scanning in CI/CD
scanScope: Helper scripts, E2E helpers, Demo code, Build config, PWA config
coverageNotes: This audit covers code provided in the codebase context. Full backend security audit requires reviewing all API routes, database access patterns, and authorization checks. Recommend running additional tools: npm audit, OWASP ZAP, Snyk
